semanticCore: Locking, sealing, closing securely, bolts, or returning caravan

keyDerivations.meaning: Lock, bolt, or sealing device that prevents opening

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ أَمْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبٍ أَقْفَالُهَا (47:24): Do they not then reflect upon the Quran, or are there locks upon their hearts?
discardedNoise: Qafilah (traveling caravan) - classical custom of calling it so in hope of safe return.
summaryEn: The root represents locking and sealing (Qafl), serving as a metaphor for spiritual blindness where hearts are bolted shut against the reception of truth.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الضم والجمع وإقفال الأبواب بالحديد وصرف الفهم (قفل)، مستخدماً أقفال القلوب مثلاً للموانع النفسية التي تحجب التدبر والهداية.
rawText: أَقْفَلَ البَابَ 4 He locked the door. (TK.) See also أَغْلَقَ. قَافِلٌ A slender horse. (TA, art. خشب.) قَافِلَةٌ A company, or an assemblage of persons, travelling together: (El-Fárábee, Msb:) or a company returning from a journey: (S, K:) and commencing a journey; as auguring their return: (El-Fárábee, Msb, K:) he who restricts it to those returning from a journey errs: (ElFárábee, Msb, in which see more:) a caravan. قَفْلٌ : see فَرَاشٌ.
